questions.text: What is the consequence of posting to an Asset Under Construction (AuC) via the Invest-WBS?
options: The AuC will be automatically deleted.
The costs are transferred from the cost object to the balance sheet.

questions.text: Who is responsible for creating Projects & WBS and assigning budgets in NEO?
What does a budget profile control in a project structure?
Where are commitments in NEO updated to differentiate them from actual postings?
options: In the Universal Journal using the ACDOCA table.

questions.text: Which statement about the codification rules for Invest-WBS is correct?
options: The codification for Invest-WBS always starts with "B."
The codification for Invest-WBS always starts with "C."
The codification for Invest-WBS always starts with "A." to distinguish it from Cost-WBS.
Invest-WBS and Cost-WBS use the same prefix in their codification.

questions.text: In NEO, all asset capitalization must use Invest-WBS, eliminating direct asset purchases.
The new Commitments Management is activated by creating an extension ledger for commitments and is updated to this ledger in NEO.

The WBS element level is where the project budget is entered 
and managed.
Invest-WBS and Cost-WBS use different codification systems to define their respective numbers in NEO.
Commitments are posted in accounting immediately upon creation.
